element_introduction_order.mastery_context: 3D camera pan from hole to starting position; overhead map provided
Variable wind affects ball trajectory; player must adjust strategy per shot
Distance and accuracy control; overhead map updates after each shot
3D contoured grid view reveals terrain variation for reading slopes
Player competes against 60 unseen opponents; scoreboard displays relative standings

typical_relief_triggers: Overhead map display after each shot
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based / self-paced; tension builds during shot execution, releases via replay and map refresh

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Practice and Driving modes available alongside Tournament

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory holes on fictional Sterling Shores or real courses with baseline wind
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Wind variance increases; green complexity heightens with course progression
difficulty_curve.late_game: Tournament scoring pressure; cumulative performance tracking across 60 opponents
notable_spikes: Transition from fairway to green (perspective/control shift)
High-difficulty real courses (PGA West, TPC Sawgrass) vs. Sterling Shores

notable_level_patterns.description: Each course/hole begins with 3D camera pan overview, progresses through fairway play with map support, culminates in contoured green view
notable_level_patterns.example_level: PGA West, TPC Avenel, TPC Sawgrass, Sterling Shores

notable_level_patterns.description: Pre-hole 3D overview → overhead map during play → grid contour on green; progressively detailed spatial data as player approaches critical zone
Good shots automatically replayed; player can manually review any shot; reduces frustration and provides learning opportunity
